lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: Human, we have given you a job to do. What else do you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need me to do again?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: Go and speak to Undor and see what he has discovered. You'll find him outside the Doors of Dinh, north of Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: ...and he says that it's of the greatest urgency.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Greatest urgency? I don't like urgency. This is quite simply not on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hiya!

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Who is this human, Councillor Unkar? What are they doing here?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't know.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh. Could you make them go away?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: Sorry human. We're currently engaging in important official business. You'll need to come back another time.

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: This is [player name]. They've been helping out in Lovakengj recently.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Well I've never heard of them. What do they want?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: What do you want human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a quest.

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: They're looking for a quest.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: A quest? Well we don't have any of those lying around.

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: Well hang on. Maybe they could help us with this urgent matter.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: What urgent matter?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: The one we were just discussing. The one with the Doors of Dinh.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Ah yes, the urgent matter with the Doors of Dinh. Very clever Councillor Unkar. You put the human to work. I need a sherry.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Jorra! Bring me a sherry.

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: Yes Lady Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: Human, we have a job for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great. What do you need?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: One of the finest smiths in Lovakengj, Undor, has been investigating issues with the Doors of Dinh. We've just received word that he has found the problem. However, he has requested we send assistance.

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: Could you go to him and see what help he needs? You'll find him right outside the Doors of Dinh, north of Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: I'm Councillor Unkar, one of the members of the Kourend Council. We are responsible for running the entire Kingdom of Great Kourend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What brings you to Lovakengj then?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: It's important for the Council to maintain a good relationship with the rulers of the five cities. Although it is the Council that rules, we can only do so with their support.

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: I'm here to help Lady Lovakengj out and to make sure she has everything she needs to rule effectively.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are the Doors of Dinh?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: They are a great set of doors built by a legendary smith, Dinh, in the early days of Kourend. They were created to imprison a deadly beast known as the Wintertodt.

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: Unfortunately, although the Doors kept the beast contained for over a thousand years, they've recently started to fail. The master smith, Undor, was sent to investigate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: They don't want anything.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh. Could you make them go away then?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: Sorry human. We're currently engaging in important official business. If you don't have anything of importance to say, you'll need to come back another time.

sections.title: Talking to Undor before talking to Ignisia

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Well hello there [laddie/lassie].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been sent to help you.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: I just need to finish this little job. I'll be with you in a minute. While you're waiting, why not talk to that fiery lass? She'll fill you in on the situation here.

sections.title: Talking to Undor after talking to Ignisia

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Hello there [laddie/lassie].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about my quest.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Have you got Dinh's Hammer yet [laddie/lassie]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. Where should I be looking?

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: I reckon it's somewhere in Dinh's Redoubt, or The Forsaken Tower, as it's now known. You'll find it west of Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Hello there [laddie/lass].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been sent to help you.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Have you now [laddie/lassie]? I was wondering when they'd send someone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well here I am. What do you need?

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: They all kept saying that the Doors were failing and I kept telling them that was rubbish. Did they listen? Of course they didn't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the doors aren't failing?

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Of course not! They were made by Dinh himself, the finest smith this world has ever seen. His work doesn't just fail!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if the Doors aren't failing, what's the issue?

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Sabotage!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sabotage? That doesn't sound good.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: You're right with that one [laddie/lassie]. Someone or something has made a small hole in the Doors.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: It's not much, but it's enough for that Wintertodt to be able to draw some power into the prison. Probably why them fiery lot have had so much trouble recently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do we do?

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Repair the damage of course [laddie/lassie]. Now I should be able to fix the issue no problem. The only thing I'm missing is the right tools. That's where you come in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What tools do you need?

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: I require a hammer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A hammer? Are you seriously telling me that you don't own a hammer?

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: What does it look like I'm holding you spoon?! I don't just need any hammer. I need the legendary hammer used by Dinh himself! It's the only tool capable of working this metal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahh, I see. So where do I find this hammer?

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Back in his prime, Dinh built a great tower. It was known as Dinh's Redoubt and it's where he created all of his finest inventions.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: After Dinh died, some poncey mage thought they'd go and defile his tower. The place has been off limits ever since.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Now I don't know for sure, but I reckon that Dinh's Hammer might still be there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well there's only one way to find out. Where do I find this tower?

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: The Forsaken Tower, as it's now known, can be found west of Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, I'll be back once I've found that hammer.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Hello there [laddie/lassie].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about my quest.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Now that I have Dinh's Hammer, it shouldn't take me long to fix the Doors. You should head back to Lady Lovakengj and let her know.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Hello there [laddie/lassie].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about my quest.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Have you got Dinh's Hammer yet [laddie/lassie]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have!

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Well hand it over then.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Look at it! Such a work of art. Dinh never fails to impress. Now that I have this, it shouldn't take me long to fix the Doors.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does this mean the Wintertodt won't be a threat anymore?

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: That should be the case in the long run. I suspect it will take a long time though. The Doors were damaged for a while and the Wintertodt will have gained a lot of power.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: I reckon that magic lot will have to do their fiery tricks for a bit longer before everything is back to normal.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Anyway, I'll get to work fixing the Doors. You should head back to Lady Lovakengj and let her know that the problem has been solved.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Undor
lines.text: Why are you wasting my time?!

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: The human seems a bit lost Councillor. Could you help them please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I've done what you asked!

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh. What did I ask?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: We asked them to go and see what help Undor needed at the Doors of Dinh.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: That we did Councillor. Quite right. And what was it that Undor needed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Undor had found some damage caused to the Doors of Dinh. He suspected someone had sabotaged them. He asked me to fetch him Dinh's Hammer so that he could fix the Doors.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found the hammer and gave it to Undor. He should be fixing the Doors as we speak.

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: Well this is excellent news. With the Doors fixed, the Wintertodt poses a far smaller threat to Kourend. Well done.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Yes, yes, wonderful stuff. I think I'll have a sherry to celebrate.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Jorra! Bring me a sherry.

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: Yes Lady Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Councillor, why is the human still here?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: I think they want a reward, Lady Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: A reward?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: Yes, Lady Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Well here you go, have this then.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh. The human is still here.
